justification: monaco-editor 0.55.1 bundles its own DOMPurify (currently 3.2.7) inside its prebundled JS files (dev/vs/editor.api-*.js, vs/editor/editor.main.js, etc). The bundled signature is what retire.js flags; our top-level dependency on dompurify is already 3.4.1 via the package.json `overrides` field, and we have a direct frontend dompurify dep at ^3.4.1 too. The advisories retire flags (GHSA-cj63-jhhr-wcxv, GHSA-cjmm-f4jc-qw8r, mXSS via reinsertion wrappers) all require the consumer code to (a) enable USE_PROFILES with the polluted prototype, (b) provide a predicate-based ADD_ATTR with EXTRA_ELEMENT_HANDLING.attributeCheck, or (c) reinsert sanitized output into a fresh parse context via innerHTML + <xmp>/<iframe>/<noembed> wrappers. Monaco does NONE of those — its internal DOMPurify usage is bounded to its own static editor-host rendering (no USE_PROFILES, no predicate ADD_ATTR, no reparse-via-wrappers). No user input reaches monaco's bundled DOMPurify in our integration. Re-audit if Monaco's vendoring strategy changes or if we expose monaco to user-supplied HTML downstream.

Next.js ships its own vendored copies of dependencies inside `next/dist/compiled/` to insulate Next from version drift in user projects. retire.js's signature scan finds these bundled libraries and flags them (currently lodash 4.17.21 inside babel-packages and jsonwebtoken bundles — GHSA-r5fr-rjxr-66jc lodash _.template code injection, GHSA-xxjr-mmjv-4gpg / GHSA-f23m-r3pf-42rh lodash prototype pollution). The advisories all require the consumer to pass UNTRUSTED INPUT into the vulnerable lodash functions: _.template with `options.imports` from user input, or _.unset/_.omit with user-controlled array paths. Next's internal lodash usage is bounded to Next's own build-time transforms (babel-packages: parsing TS/JSX; jsonwebtoken: JWT signing — both with developer-controlled, static inputs). No user input reaches lodash._template or lodash._.unset/_.omit array paths via Next's bundled copies in our integration. Same allowlist logic as the top-level npm-audit gate (GHSA-r5fr-rjxr-66jc and GHSA-xxjr-mmjv-4gpg are already documented in scripts/ci/check-frontend-audit.py). Re-audit if Next changes to call _.template with user-supplied options.imports OR if we surface raw lodash-handled paths from user input to Next's internals.
